g.text: Client (browser / curl)
DNS resolves → public IP of Load Balancer. TCP handshake begins.
Cloud Load Balancer (external)
Managed by cloud provider (AWS ELB, GCP LB…). Health-checks nodes.
Picks a healthy Node IP, forwards to NodePort (e.g. 31456).
Node — network interface (eth0)
Packet arrives at the node's physical/virtual NIC. Kernel receives it.
Passes through kernel netfilter chains (PREROUTING first).
kube-proxy — iptables / IPVS rules (DNAT)
Matches: dest port 31456 → this is a NodePort Service.
DNAT: rewrites destination from NodeIP:31456 → ClusterIP:80.
Then immediately resolves ClusterIP → one of the Endpoint PodIPs.
ClusterIP — virtual IP (no physical hop)
ClusterIP is NOT a real device. It exists only as an iptables rule target.
Traffic is DNAT'd straight to a selected PodIP — ClusterIP is never seen on wire.

svg.text: routed to PodIP (same or diff node)

g.text: CNI plugin — pod network (overlay or underlay)
If Pod is on same node: packet goes via veth pair directly into Pod netns.
If Pod is on different node: CNI tunnels packet (VXLAN/BGP) to target node.
Pod — eth0 (veth inside Pod netns)
Packet arrives at Pod's virtual ethernet interface.
Kernel delivers to process listening on containerPort (e.g. 8080).
App container (your code)
HTTP request lands at your FastAPI / Express / Django app on port 8080.
Response travels the exact same path in reverse — SNAT undoes the DNAT.
